allies.rationale_text: Incentives explain whether signals are costly enough to be trustworthy or cheap enough to reward appearance over substance.
allies.source_quote: cheap symbolic signals are rewarded over costly truth-aligned behavior

allies.rationale_text: Principal-agent-problem clarifies why signaling matters: the actor sending the signal may benefit from looking trustworthy even when underlying quality is weak.
allies.source_quote: appearance-trustworthy behavior rather than be-trustworthy behavior

allies.rationale_text: Abstraction helps signaling compress a complex reality into a legible form without forcing the receiver through every underlying detail.
Commitment-and-consistency-bias becomes stronger when signaling turns a private view into a public identity marker, making later reversal feel reputationally costly.
allies.source_quote: publicly observable signals can bind future behavior by making reversal more expensive

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias degrades signaling when receivers filter evidence toward a preferred conclusion regardless of signal quality.
antagonists.source_quote: accurate signals to fail regardless of their quality

structured_tensions.tension_text: Signaling conflicts with incentives when cheap symbolic signals are rewarded over costly truth-aligned behavior.
structured_tensions.source_quote: signaling vs incentives: signaling conflicts with incentives (incentives) when cheap symbolic signals are rewarded over costly truth-aligned behavior.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Signaling conflicts with confirmation-bias when the receiver filters incoming signals toward a preferred conclusion, causing accurate signals to fail regardless of their quality.
structured_tensions.source_quote: signaling vs confirmation-bias: signaling conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when the receiver filters incoming signals toward a preferred conclusion, causing accurate signals to fail regardless of their quality.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on incentives, principal-agent distortion, and an explicit conflict with cheap-symbolic and confirmation-biased signal failure, so the file preserves that conservative surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation widening beyond the clearly named incentive, agency, and anti-confirmation neighborhood.
open_questions: Optionality-like commitment pressure is source-real and is now partially wired through commitment-and-consistency-bias, but future sweeps may still sharpen the exact public-identity language.
